questions.text: The distance of a point P(x, y) from the origin O(0, 0) is given by:
questions.explanation: Using the distance formula: √((x-0)² + (y-0)²) = √(x² + y²).

questions.text: The distance between the points A(0, 6) and B(0, -2) is:
questions.explanation: Since both points are on the y-axis, the distance is |6 - (-2)| = 8 units.

questions.text: The distance of the point P(2, 3) from the x-axis is:
questions.explanation: The distance of a point from the x-axis is its y-coordinate (ordinate).

questions.text: The distance of the point P(-4, -7) from the y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Distance is always positive. The distance from the y-axis is the absolute value of the x-coordinate.

questions.text: If the distance between the points (x, -1) and (3, 2) is 5, then the value of x is:
questions.explanation: Using distance formula: (x-3)² + (2 - (-1))² = 5². (x-3)² + 9 = 25 => (x-3)² = 16 => x-3 = ±4.

questions.text: The coordinates of the mid-point of the line segment joining the points P(x₁, y₁) and Q(x₂, y₂) are:
questions.explanation: The mid-point formula averages the x-coordinates and the y-coordinates.

questions.text: The mid-point of the line segment joining A(2, 6) and B(4, 4) is:
The point which divides the line segment joining the points (1, 2) and (3, 4) internally in the ratio 1 : 1 is:
questions.explanation: A ratio of 1:1 means the point is the mid-point: ((1+3)/2, (2+4)/2) = (2, 3).

questions.text: Any point on the x-axis is of the form:
questions.explanation: On the x-axis, the y-coordinate (ordinate) is always zero.

questions.text: Any point on the y-axis is of the form:
questions.explanation: On the y-axis, the x-coordinate (abscissa) is always zero.

questions.text: If the points A(6, 1), B(8, 2), C(9, 4) and D(p, 3) are the vertices of a parallelogram, taken in order, find the value of p.
questions.explanation: Diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other. Mid-point of AC = Mid-point of BD. (6+9)/2 = (8+p)/2 => 15 = 8+p => p=7.

questions.text: The ratio in which the y-axis divides the line segment joining the points (5, -6) and (-1, -4) is:
questions.explanation: On the y-axis, x=0. Using section formula for x: 0 = (m₁(-1) + m₂(5)) / (m₁+m₂) => m₁ = 5m₂ => m₁/m₂ = 5/1.

questions.text: The coordinates of the point which divides the join of (-1, 7) and (4, -3) in the ratio 2 : 3 is:
The distance between A(x₁, y₁) and B(x₂, y₂) is:
questions.explanation: This is the standard distance formula derived from the Pythagoras theorem.
By checking the slopes or distances (AB+BC=AC), these points do not lie on a single straight line.

questions.text: The distance of the point P(-6, 8) from the origin is:
If the mid-point of the line segment joining A(3, 4) and B(k, 6) is P(x, y) and x + y - 10 = 0, find k.
The perimeter of a triangle with vertices (0, 4), (0, 0) and (3, 0) is:
questions.explanation: Side lengths are 4, 3, and √(3²+4²)=5. Perimeter = 4 + 3 + 5 = 12.

questions.text: The ratio in which the line segment joining the points (4, 8) and (3, -12) is divided by the x-axis is:
The coordinates of the centroid of a triangle with vertices (x₁, y₁), (x₂, y₂) and (x₃, y₃) are:
questions.explanation: The centroid is the point of intersection of the medians, calculated by the average of coordinates.

questions.text: Centroid of a triangle whose vertices are (0, 6), (8, 12) and (8, 0) is:
If A(5, 3), B(11, -5) and P(12, y) are the vertices of a right triangle right-angled at B, then y is:
questions.explanation: Using slope(AB) * slope(BP) = -1 or Pythagoras theorem. Calculating gives y = -4.

questions.text: The points A(-4, 0), B(4, 0), C(0, 3) are the vertices of a:
questions.explanation: AC = √((-4-0)² + (0-3)²) = 5. BC = √((4-0)² + (0-3)²) = 5. Since AC = BC, it is isosceles.

questions.text: If the point P(2, 1) lies on the line segment joining points A(4, 2) and B(8, 4), then:
questions.explanation: Calculating distances: AP = √((2-4)² + (1-2)²) = √5 and AB = √((8-4)² + (4-2)²) = √20 = 2√5. Since AP is exactly half of AB, AP = 1/2 AB.

questions.text: If P(a/3, 4) is the mid-point of the line segment joining the points Q(-6, 5) and R(-2, 3), then the value of a is:
The perpendicular bisector of the line segment joining the points A(1, 5) and B(4, 6) cuts the y-axis at:
questions.explanation: Any point on y-axis is (0, y). It's equidistant from A and B. (0-1)² + (y-5)² = (0-4)² + (y-6)². 1 + y² - 10y + 25 = 16 + y² - 12y + 36 => 2y = 26 => y = 13.

questions.text: The points A(9, 0), B(9, 6), C(-9, 6) and D(-9, 0) are the vertices of a:
questions.explanation: Adjacent sides are perpendicular and unequal (lengths 6 and 18).

questions.text: Distance between (a cos θ + b sin θ, 0) and (0, a sin θ - b cos θ) is:
questions.explanation: D² = (a cos θ + b sin θ)² + (a sin θ - b cos θ)². Expanding gives a²(cos²θ + sin²θ) + b²(sin²θ + cos²θ) = a² + b².

questions.text: The line segment joining points (-3, -4) and (1, -2) is divided by y-axis in the ratio:
questions.explanation: On y-axis, x=0. 0 = (m₁(1) + m₂(-3))/(m₁+m₂) => m₁ = 3m₂ => m₁/m₂ = 3/1.

questions.text: If A(2, 2), B(-4, -4) and C(5, -8) are vertices of a triangle, the length of the median through C is:
The point on the x-axis which is equidistant from (-1, 0) and (5, 0) is:
questions.explanation: Mid-point of the two points on the x-axis: ((-1+5)/2, 0) = (2, 0).

questions.text: The fourth vertex D of a parallelogram ABCD whose three vertices are A(-2, 3), B(6, 7) and C(8, 3) is:
questions.explanation: Mid-point of AC = ((-2+8)/2, (3+3)/2) = (3, 3). This must be mid-point of BD: (6+x)/2 = 3 => x=0; (7+y)/2 = 3 => y=-1.

questions.text: If the distance between (4, p) and (1, 0) is 5, then p is:
The coordinates of the points of trisection of the line segment joining (2, -2) and (-7, 4) are:
questions.explanation: Points dividing in 1:2 and 2:1. Using section formula gives these results.

questions.text: If the points A(1, 2), O(0, 0) and C(a, b) are collinear, then:
questions.explanation: collinearity with origin implies ratio of coordinates is same: 2/1 = b/a => b = 2a.

questions.text: The distance between the points (a sin θ, a cos θ) and (a cos θ, -a sin θ) is:
questions.explanation: D² = (a sin θ - a cos θ)² + (a cos θ + a sin θ)² = 2a²(sin²θ + cos²θ) = 2a².

questions.text: If the points (0, 0), (1, 2) and (x, y) are collinear, then:
A circle drawn with origin as centre passes through (13/2, 0). The point which does not lie in the interior of the circle is:
questions.explanation: Radius is 6.5. Distance of (-6, 2.5) from origin is √(36 + 6.25) = √42.25 = 6.5. It lies ON the circle, not interior.

questions.text: If A(m/3, 5) is the mid-point of line segment joining Q(-6, 7) and R(-2, 3), then m is:
Ratio in which the line 3x + y - 9 = 0 divides the segment joining (1, 3) and (2, 7) is:
